name: Ascent to Alto de Gayubar — circular from Vallejimeno
name_original: Subida al Alto de Gayubar — circular desde Vallejimeno
region_intro: Jogging routes around Valle De Valdelaguna offer diverse natural landscapes within the Sierra de la Demanda, characterized by mountains, forests, and glacial lagoons. The region features rolling hills and valleys, providing varied terrain for physical activity. Trails often traverse forested areas and meadows, with some leading to higher altitudes and offering panoramic views. This environment supports a range of running experiences, from paths through serene woodlands to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. Best jogging routes around Valle De Valdelaguna The most popular jogging route is Lagunas de Neila Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the scenic glacial lagoons of Neila. Another top favourite among local runners is Las Lagunas de Neila and ascent to Alto de la Campiña, a difficult 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route offers a circular journey through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space, combining lagoon views with an ascent to a prominent peak. Local runners also love the Ascent to Alto de Gayubar — circular from Vallejimeno, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Jogging around Valle De Valdelaguna is defined by glacial lagoons, the rolling hills of the Sierra de la Demanda, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle paths to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Valle De Valdelaguna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Valle De Valdelaguna?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Valle De Valdelaguna listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de la Demanda, from forested paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any easy jogging trails for beginners in Valle De Valdelaguna?
A: While many routes in Valle De Valdelaguna are rated as moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de la Demanda, there are paths suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run. For example, sections around the &lt;strong&gt;Neila Lagoons&lt;/strong&gt; offer relatively flatter terrain, though dedicated easy routes are less common. It's always recommended to check the route profile for elevation changes before heading out.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running routes in Valle De Valdelaguna?
A: Runners in Valle De Valdelaguna can expect breathtaking natural scenery. The routes traverse diverse landscapes including dense pine forests, open meadows, and rolling hills. A major highlight is the stunning glacial &lt;strong&gt;Neila Lagoons&lt;/strong&gt;, which offer impressive visual spectacles, especially during autumn. You'll also encounter panoramic views from higher elevations within the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Valle De Valdelaguna offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050028450/las-lagunas-de-neila-and-ascent-to-alto-de-la-campina-circular-through-the-sierra-de-la-demanda-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lagunas de Neila and ascent to Alto de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a challenging loop through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space, combining lagoon views with a climb to a prominent peak. Another great circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050059840/ascent-to-alto-de-gayubar-circular-from-vallejimeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Alto de Gayubar — circular from Vallejimeno&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints along the jogging routes?
A: Many running routes offer access to significant natural landmarks and stunning viewpoints. The &lt;strong&gt;Neila Lagoons&lt;/strong&gt; themselves are a major attraction, with a dedicated viewpoint providing panoramic vistas. For those seeking higher elevations, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3856385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campiña&lt;/a&gt; offers breathtaking views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico San Millán (Torruco)&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in Burgos province, provides rewarding views after the climb.

Q: Is Valle De Valdelaguna suitable for trail running with dogs?
A: The natural environment of Valle De Valdelaguna, with its extensive forests and open spaces, can be very enjoyable for running with dogs. However, it's crucial to always keep your dog under control, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Check local regulations for specific trails, and ensure your dog is well-behaved and on a leash where required to protect the natural habitat and other users.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in Valle De Valdelaguna?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, some sections, particularly around the &lt;strong&gt;Neila Lagoons&lt;/strong&gt;, offer more accessible paths that families might enjoy for a lighter jog or walk. When planning a family outing, look for routes with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances. Always review the route details and difficulty before setting out with children.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Valle De Valdelaguna?
A: The best time for jogging in Valle De Valdelaguna largely depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, with autumn providing spectacular views around the &lt;strong&gt;Neila Lagoons&lt;/strong&gt; during the thaw. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are advisable. Winter running is possible but requires appropriate gear for colder temperatures and potential snow, especially at higher altitudes.

Q: Are there routes that offer a good challenge for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de la Demanda provides excellent challenges for experienced runners. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050026036/lagunas-de-neila-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Neila Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050059840/ascent-to-alto-de-gayubar-circular-from-vallejimeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Alto de Gayubar — circular from Vallejimeno&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain, making them ideal for those seeking a demanding trail running experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Many trailheads in Valle De Valdelaguna, especially those leading to popular areas like the &lt;strong&gt;Neila Lagoons&lt;/strong&gt;, typically have designated parking areas. For specific routes, it's best to check the route details on komoot, as parking information is often provided by other users or in the route description. Access points to natural parks or protected areas may have specific parking regulations.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or cultural points of interest?
A: While Valle De Valdelaguna is primarily known for its natural beauty, some routes may pass near or through villages with local charm. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050049770/castillo-de-castrovido-circular-from-salas-de-los-infantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Castrovido — circular from Salas de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt; route offers a glimpse of historical elements, starting from Salas de los Infantes and potentially passing by the ruins of Castrovido Castle. The region's history is often intertwined with its natural resources, such as former mining railway lines now used as nature paths.
tips: In the valley of the Tejero river, on the Atlantic slope of the Sierra de la Demanda and to the north of the Sierra de Neila, this small but pretty town called Vallejimeno appears. A Roman bridge appears that crosses the river!
In one of the bends before reaching the top of Alto de Gayubar we find a high watch tower that helps in dry times to monitor the set of slopes that are around the area.

From this point you can see impressive panoramic views of the whole of the valleys that appear around, where at the top of the Sierra de Neila, there is the Alto de Gayubar that is above one thousand six hundred meters height
